newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6. Stock drifted from ~Rs576 (Jun 5) to Rs542.5 (Jun 8) and is roughly flat today (-0.36%), still digesting the weak Q4 FY26 print (net revenue Rs333 cr, -83% YoY; Rs211 cr loss). Known but not-new positives: commercial launch of BSNL's pan-India 4G network, selection as IP/MPLS router OEM for BharatNet Phase III packages, and a pending BSNL add-on PO (~18,000 sites, ~Rs1,526 cr) with inventory pre-staged. AGM scheduled June 26 (e-voting Jun 22-25).

sources.label: INDmoney - Tejas Networks share price, Q4 FY26 results, BharatNet Phase III and AGM dates
Trade Brains - Tejas Networks: turnaround or value trap after BSNL slowdown (FY26 revenue Rs1,103 cr vs Rs8,923 cr FY25; pending 18,000-site add-on PO)
Voice&Data - Tejas Networks posts FY26 loss amid BSNL order delays

verdicts.rankRationale: No new information since yesterday's hold; today's -0.36% is noise and the sovereign build-out thesis (BSNL 4G/5G, BharatNet Phase III) is unchanged with the binary BSNL add-on PO still pending. Last week's ~6% pullback was already reflected in the June 8 verdict, so rank 42 stays.
verdicts.bull: Pending BSNL add-on PO (~18,000 sites, ~Rs1,526 cr) with inventory pre-staged means revenue can restart quickly, and BharatNet Phase III router OEM selection plus the NEC mMIMO export deal extend the sovereign-networking runway at a $1B cap.
verdicts.neutral: FY26 collapsed to Rs1,103 cr revenue with quarterly losses; the stock is a sovereign-policy option whose value hinges almost entirely on government order timing, which has already slipped ~8 months.
verdicts.bear: Further BSNL/BharatNet order delays keep the P&L deeply loss-making, inventory and debt pressure mount, and the +20% YTD bounce unwinds toward bearish analyst targets near Rs460.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Conversion of the BSNL advance purchase order into a final ~Rs1,526 cr add-on PO (plus BharatNet Phase III execution); AGM June 26 as a near-term information event.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued slippage of government order finalization, leaving revenue down >80% YoY with sustained losses and inventory/working-capital strain.
